top_signals.title: Oil Surges as Trump Says US Will Be Paid 20% for Hormuz Flows
top_signals.summary: Oil jumped after US President Donald Trump said he would reimpose a blockade on Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demand a payment for all other cargo moving through the waterway, dashing hopes for a near-term recovery in shipping flows.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Dubai Is Already Planning for the Next Strait of Hormuz Crisis
top_signals.summary: The Strait of Hormuz reopened less than a month ago, but Dubai is already planning for the next closure. DP World is reportedly in talks to build a new multipurpose port and container terminal in Fujairah on the UAE's east coast, creating another shipping route that avoids Hormuz altogether. According to the Financial Times, construction could take as little as 18 months. The company has acknowledged it is pursuing diversification projects to get through future disruptions. That timeline says plenty. Ports are built to solve problems measured in…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Trump administration urges banks to scrutinize lending to immigrants without work authorization
top_signals.summary: Federal bank regulators issued guidance Monday seeking to curb mortgages, auto loans and other consumer credit to immigrants without U.S. work authorization.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.
